summary: Graduate scoreAssumptionHandling: require explicit assumption-conflict language, not just keyword overlap with task output
context: scoreAssumptionHandling was scoring 100 across all conditions because every agent that builds an SMS adapter naturally produces text matching "preference", "sms", "channel", and routing patterns. The scorer could not discriminate between agents that genuinely identified the email-only assumption conflict vs agents that merely wrote SMS code.
rationale: Three-component graduated scorer: (1) Session 8 flag (+40 max): strong signal requires explicit assumption-conflict language (assum.*email-only, hardcoded.*email, etc.), weak signal gives only 15 pts for generic preference mentions. (2) Session 9 preferences restructure (+35): checks for structural patterns (channel types, channel arrays) with minimum diff size >100 chars, not just keyword presence in a preferences file. (3) Session 9 routing (+25 max, reduced from 35): requires preference-based routing (preference.*channel + switch/if/forEach patterns), basic multi-channel gets only 10. Total still 100 max but achieving it requires genuinely handling the assumption change.

alternatives.option: Keep existing scorer, add a stricter session 8 pattern only
alternatives.reason_rejected: Session 9 patterns also match generically — any agent adding SMS will touch a preferences file and use 'sms' and 'channel' keywords. Both components need fixing.
Adds cost and non-determinism per run. Automated patterns can detect genuine assumption handling from diffs. LLM judge can be added as a refinement later.

alternatives.option: Remove the dimension and redistribute weight
alternatives.reason_rejected: The dimension measures an important coordination quality (response to mid-sprint requirement changes). The scorer logic was broken, not the concept.

assumptions: Session 8 task prompt asks agents to flag assumptions that need updating
Session 9 task involves restructuring the notification system for multi-channel
Agents that genuinely handle the assumption will use language like 'email-only assumption', 'hardcoded email', or 'preferences need updating for multi-channel'
